codeReview.summary: The implementation successfully rewrote ChangelogPage from localStorage-based storage to backend API integration with full CRUD support (create, edit, delete) and proper loading/error state handling. The changelogApi wrapper in adminApi.ts correctly implements the backend contract with POST/PUT/DELETE /admin/changelog endpoints and GET /changelog for reading. Test coverage is comprehensive with 20 tests covering all CRUD operations, loading/error states, and WhatsNewBanner behavior. ExportApprovalsPage was already correctly wired and its 10 tests remain passing. The code is maintainable, follows project conventions, and aligns with the backend contract.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The changelogApi pattern demonstrates a clean separation between admin write endpoints (/admin/changelog for POST/PUT/DELETE) and public read endpoint (/changelog for GET). This pattern could be documented in api.md for future API wrapper implementations.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/lib/adminApi.ts:changelogApi — admin endpoints use /admin prefix, public read uses /changelog. Similar pattern used in other admin API wrappers.

summary: PASS. The feature correctly implements changelog management with backend API integration, replacing the previous localStorage-only approach. The changelogApi wrapper properly follows the backend contract with correct endpoint paths (/admin/changelog for writes, /changelog for reads). Full CRUD is implemented with proper loading/error states, isHighlighted support, and toast notifications. Test coverage is thorough (30 tests total: 20 changelog + 10 export-approvals). ExportApprovalsPage was already correctly wired and required no changes. No blocking issues found.
